data.title: Finland's Nokia Q1 net sales rise 4% on strong Optical Networks growth
data.description:
Overview

  • Finland connectivity provider's Q1 2026 net sales grew 4% yr/yr on constant currency basis

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 2026 rose to EUR 0.05

  • Company saw strong Optical Networks growth and expanded gross margin

Outlook

  • Nokia maintains full-year 2026 comparable operating profit guidance of EUR 2.0-2.5 bln

  • Company expects 2026 Network Infrastructure net sales to grow 12-14%, with IP and Optical Networks up 18-20%

  • Nokia assumes Q2 net sales to rise 5-9% q-o-q and Q2 operating profit to be 12-16% of full-year

Result Drivers

  • OPTICAL NETWORKS GROWTH - Optical Networks net sales grew 20%, driven by strong order intake and new design wins, per CEO Justin Hotard

  • AI & CLOUD DEMAND - Net sales from AI & Cloud customers grew 49%, with EUR 1 bln in orders booked, reflecting accelerating demand, per CEO Justin Hotard

  • PRODUCT MIX SHIFT - Fixed Networks net sales declined 13% due to a strategic shift to higher-margin products, per CEO Justin Hotard

Company press release:

  • The average consensus recommendation for the communications & networking peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Nokia Oyj is €6.78, about 20.4% below its April 22 closing price of €8.51

  • The stock recently traded at 25 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 17 three months ago

data.title: Belgium's Azelis Q1 profit dips, sees uplift from geopolitical tensions more moderate than post-Covid
data.description:
Overview

  • Belgium specialty chemicals distributor's Q1 revenue fell 0.7% yr/yr in constant currency

  • Adjusted EBITA declined 7.9% yr/yr in constant currency, with margin contracting to 10% from 10.9%

  • Company maintained strong free cash flow despite volatile market conditions

Outlook

  • Azelis says near-term market conditions continue to limit visibility on pace of recovery

  • Company expects any uplift from current geopolitical tensions to be more moderate than post-Covid

  • Azelis remains focused on cost discipline and strong cash generation amid market volatility

Result Drivers

  • ORGANIC DECLINE OFFSET BY ACQUISITIONS - Revenue fell as a 3.9% organic decline, mainly in EMEA, was partly offset by a 3.3% contribution from acquisitions

  • NEGATIVE MIX EFFECTS - Gross profit margin contracted due to negative mix effects across the group, notably in Asia Pacific where market weakness and competitive pressure persisted

  • COST DISCIPLINE - Cost discipline and lower operating costs partially mitigated the impact of lower revenue and margin pressures

Company press release:

data.title: Finland's Verkkokauppa Q1 revenue rises less than expected
data.description:
Overview

  • Finland online electronics retailer's Q1 revenue grew 6.7% yr/yr but missed analyst expectations

  • Gross margin fell to 17.3% from 18.8% due to tough comparison and divestment impact

  • Company maintained 2026 guidance for revenue and comparable EBIT growth amid market uncertainty

Outlook

  • Verkkokauppa.com expects 2026 revenue and comparable operating result to increase from 2025

  • Company says guidance includes uncertainties related to consumer purchasing power and behavior

  • Verkkokauppa.com notes results depend largely on fourth-quarter sales due to seasonality

Result Drivers

  • HOME APPLIANCES AND IT GROWTH - Revenue growth was primarily driven by strong performance in home appliances and IT categories, supported by the ongoing computer replacement cycle

  • INTERNATIONAL SALES - International sales rose significantly, with particularly strong growth in Sweden

  • MARGIN PRESSURE - Gross margin declined due to a tough comparison period, divestment of the consumer financing business, and intensified price competition amid softer consumer demand

Company press release:

data.title: STMicroelectronics reports Q1 earnings above expectations
data.description:
STMicroelectronics on Thursday reported first-quarter results above estimates pointing to signs of recovery in its key semiconductor markets.

The Franco-Italian chipmaker reported revenue of $3.10 billion for the first three months of the year, versus the $3.04 billion expected by analysts polled by LSEG. Operating income was $171 million, above market expectations of $165.8 million.

data.title: France's Ipsen Q1 revenue rises 17%, rare disease sales more than double
data.description:
Overview

  • France biopharma firm's Q1 revenue rose 17% as reported, driven by all therapeutic areas

  • Rare disease sales more than doubled, led by Iqirvo and Bylvay performance

  • Company confirmed full-year 2026 guidance for sales growth and operating margin

Outlook

  • Ipsen confirms 2026 guidance for total sales growth greater than 13% at constant currency

  • Company expects core operating margin greater than 35% of total sales for 2026

  • Ipsen anticipates adverse currency impact of around 1% on total sales in 2026

Result Drivers

  • RARE DISEASE FRANCHISE - Growth in Rare Disease sales, particularly from Iqirvo and Bylvay, contributed significantly to Q1 results, per CEO David Loew

  • BROAD-BASED SALES GROWTH - All three therapeutic areas contributed to total sales growth, with portfolio momentum accelerating outside Somatuline

Company press release:

data.title: Stecon Group Faces Gross Margin Hit From High Fuel Prices — Market Talk
data.description: Stecon Group faces a hit to gross margins from high fuel prices, CGS International's Kasem Prunratanamala says in a research report. High oil prices pressure construction contractors such as Stecon Group through direct fuel costs and the "cascade" effect on oil-derived materials, the analyst says. This could materially compress the Thai company's margins and delay project activity. The brokerage cuts its gross margin assumptions for the company to 6.3% from 7.0% for 2026 and to 6.6% from 7.0% for 2027. It downgrades the stock to hold from add and lowers the target price to 12.10 baht from 13.50 baht. Shares are 3.3% lower at 11.70 baht. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Switzerland's Nestle Q1 organic sales rises, back FY forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • Switzerland-based food and beverage giant's Q1 organic growth was 3.5%, led by coffee and snacks

  • Reported sales fell 5.7% yr/yr, mainly due to negative foreign exchange impacts

  • Infant formula recall weighed on Q1 organic growth by about 90 bps

Outlook

  • Nestle maintains 2026 organic growth guidance at around 3% to 4%

  • Company expects 2026 UTOP margin to improve versus 2025, strengthening in H2

  • Nestle sees 2026 free cash flow above CHF 9 bln

Result Drivers

  • COFFEE GROWTH - Coffee was the primary growth driver, with 9.3% organic growth led by pricing and supported by higher volumes, especially in Nescafé

  • INFANT FORMULA RECALL - Infant formula recall weighed on organic growth and real internal growth, particularly in Nutrition, with product availability now back to normal

  • EMERGING MARKETS - Growth in emerging markets stood out, with organic growth of 6.8% and real internal growth of 2.9% in emerging markets excluding China

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

Q1 Organic Growth

Beat/Miss

Actual

3.50%

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 11 "strong buy" or "buy", 12 "hold" and 3 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the food processing peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Nestle SA is CHF85.00, about 12.5% above its April 22 closing price of CHF75.54

  • The stock recently traded at 17 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 17 three months ago
